DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata rich-text status usecase
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api artifact-reference best-practice design-specifications guideline interaction principle purpose scale state variant

DSDS Anatomy Guideline

Documents the visual structure of a component — its named sub-elements (parts), their associated design tokens, and optional visual previews. Anatomy guidelines decompose a component into the discrete parts that designers and engineers need to understand, style, and test.

Source: guidelines/anatomy.schema.json

2 definitions in this file:

  • anatomyEntry
  • anatomy

anatomyEntry

A named sub-element of a component's visual structure.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredMachine-readable name of the part (e.g., 'container', 'label', 'icon', 'focus-ring').
descriptionrichTextrequiredWhat this part is, what it does, and any constraints on its content or appearance.
requiredbooleanrequiredWhether this part is always present in the component's rendered output. When false, the part is conditionally rendered based on props or content.
displayNamestringoptionalHuman-readable name (e.g., 'Container', 'Label', 'Leading Icon').
tokensstring[]optionalNames of design tokens applied to this part (e.g., 'button-background', 'button-border-radius', 'button-padding-horizontal'). Connects the visual structure to the token architecture.
linkslink[]optionalLinks to resources that represent this anatomy part — design tool nodes, source code blocks, documentation sections, or any other addressable reference.

References: richText, link

Example

[
  {
    "name": "container",
    "displayName": "Container",
    "description": "The outer wrapper element that defines the button's clickable area and visual boundary.",
    "required": true,
    "tokens": [
      "button-background",
      "button-border-color",
      "button-border-radius",
      "button-padding-horizontal",
      "button-padding-vertical"
    ],
    "links": [
      {
        "type": "source",
        "url": "https://code.acme.com/design-system/src/components/button/button.tsx#L42-L58",
        "label": "Source — container element"
      },
      {
        "type": "design",
        "url": "https://design-tool.acme.com/file/abc123?node-id=1234:5678",
        "label": "Design node — container"
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "label",
    "displayName": "Label",
    "description": "The text content of the button. Communicates the action that will occur when activated.",
    "required": true,
    "tokens": [
      "button-text-color",
      "button-font-size",
      "button-font-weight",
      "button-line-height"
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "icon",
    "displayName": "Icon",
    "description": "An optional icon displayed before (leading) or after (trailing) the label. Reinforces the label's meaning visually.",
    "required": false,
    "tokens": [
      "button-icon-size",
      "button-icon-color",
      "button-icon-gap"
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "focus-ring",
    "displayName": "Focus Ring",
    "description": "A visible outline that appears when the button receives keyboard focus. Must be visible in all color modes.",
    "required": false,
    "tokens": [
      "button-focus-ring-color",
      "button-focus-ring-width",
      "button-focus-ring-offset"
    ]
  }
]

anatomy

Documents the visual structure of a component by enumerating its named sub-elements (parts). Each part can reference the design tokens that style it, creating a bridge between the component's visual design and its token architecture. An optional preview provides an annotated diagram of the assembled structure.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
type"anatomy"requiredIdentifies this guideline as an anatomy specification.
partsanatomyEntry[]requiredThe named sub-elements of the component, in visual order (typically outside-in or top-to-bottom). Each part documents a discrete element that can be independently styled, tested, or referenced.
Min items: 1
descriptionrichTextoptionalA prose overview of the component's visual structure — how the parts relate to each other and any notable structural constraints.
previewexample[]optionalVisual previews of the anatomy — typically annotated diagrams with numbered callouts mapping to the parts array. Multiple previews allow different views (e.g., expanded vs. collapsed, with icon vs. without).

References: richText, anatomyEntry, example

Example

{
  "description": "The Button is composed of a container, a text label, and an optional leading or trailing icon. A focus ring appears on keyboard focus.",
  "parts": [
    {
      "name": "container",
      "displayName": "Container",
      "description": "The outer wrapper element that defines the button's clickable area and visual boundary.",
      "required": true,
      "tokens": [
        "button-background",
        "button-border-color",
        "button-border-radius"
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "label",
      "displayName": "Label",
      "description": "The text content of the button.",
      "required": true,
      "tokens": [
        "button-text-color",
        "button-font-size"
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "icon",
      "displayName": "Icon",
      "description": "An optional icon displayed before or after the label.",
      "required": false,
      "tokens": [
        "button-icon-size",
        "button-icon-color"
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "focus-ring",
      "displayName": "Focus Ring",
      "description": "A visible outline that appears when the button receives keyboard focus.",
      "required": false,
      "tokens": [
        "button-focus-ring-color",
        "button-focus-ring-width"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "type": "anatomy"
}
↑ Back to top
